dependencies: Third-party suppliers for equipment, materials, and services
Outsourcing of services including information technology, transaction processing, human resources, payroll
Global supply chain for specialized equipment with long lead times
Interstate pipelines for natural gas supply (Tennessee Gas Pipeline Company, Algonquin Gas Transmission Company)
Water supplies from owned surface water sources and groundwater supplies
risks: Dependence on external suppliers for materials and equipment
Disruptions in delivery of electricity, natural gas and water
Rising geo-political tensions could negatively impact the global supply chain
Substandard performance or inability to fulfill obligations by third-party suppliers and service providers
Contractor performance issues could affect service reliability
Supply constraints in New England leading to significant increases in commodity costs
Supply chain disruptions could impact construction projects and system maintenance
Global supply chain disruptions or inflationary impacts

regulatory_risks: FERC Supplemental NOPR on Transmission Incentives proposing to eliminate existing RTO ROE incentive adder
Changes in tax laws and potential tax effects of business decisions
Compliance with nuclear waste disposal regulations and DOE obligations
Requirements for purchases of renewable energy credits with increasing costs and alternative compliance payments
Massachusetts Future of Gas docket (DPU 20-80) requiring new processes for natural gas investments
Connecticut statute requiring electric utilities to file distribution rate case within four years
Massachusetts climate compliance plans required every five years beginning April 2025
Disallowance of incurred costs by regulators
Regulatory approval requirements for rate increases and capital investments
Massachusetts DPU requirements for electric distribution companies to file distribution rate schedules every five years
Rate regulation by state regulatory commissions (PURA, DPU, NHPUC) affecting cost recovery and returns
Potential disallowance of storm restoration costs by regulators
Changes in environmental regulations affecting operations and costs
Regulatory decisions requiring cancellation, reduction, or delay of planned development activities or capital expenditures
Massachusetts natural gas LDCs required to file distribution rate schedules every 10 years
Water quality regulations compliance (PFAS and lead treatment, customer lead service lines replacement)
State energy policy mandates requiring renewable energy purchases
Changes in tax laws and regulations affecting deferred tax assets
Regulatory approval required for sale of Aquarion water business
Potential penalties for failure to meet performance standards
Regulatory review of prudency of costs incurred
Compliance with environmental laws and regulations
Regulatory mechanisms affecting revenue decoupling and cost recovery
Connecticut law prohibiting CL&P's ESI capital tracking mechanism reauthorization
PURA performance-based regulation (PBR) implementation for electric distribution companies
EPA final regulation setting drinking water standards for six PFAS compounds with phased compliance approach
Actions or inaction of local, state and federal regulatory, public policy and taxing bodies
New Hampshire NHPUC rate review discretion and two-year investigation limitation
Costs of compliance with environmental laws and regulations, including those related to climate change
Compliance with FERC ROE complaint proceedings and potential refunds
FERC changes to methodology on developing ROEs
More stringent laws and regulations governing operations (reliability and customer service quality standards, vegetation management requirements)
Changes in laws, regulations, Presidential executive orders or regulatory policy
FERC regulation of transmission rates and wholesale electricity markets
Downward adjustments in allowed ROE and assessment of penalties and fines by regulators
Competition for transmission projects within existing service territories
FERC policy changes regarding transmission rate incentives (RTO participation ROE incentive adder, CWIP in rate base incentive, abandoned plant incentive)
Regulatory scrutiny of performance standards and service quality
